title: podman save with multiple images generates invalid metadata
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

As a follow-up of #9590 I've switched to the default save format, which appeared to work (`podman save image1 image2 | podman load` lists both images as being imported), but upon closer inspection the images are "merged" and only one is actually loaded with all tags pointing to it.
Manually writing manifests and registry works for load, so this is purely a save problem.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

0. have two images, I only tried with images on top of each other like this:
```
$ podman image tree my_image:1.1
Image ID: 3a6990719716
Tags:     [localhost/my_image:1.1]
Size:     221.1MB
Image Layers
├──  ID: cb381a32b229 Size:  5.88MB Top Layer of: [docker.io/amd64/alpine:latest docker.io/library/alpine:latest]
├──  ID: b53bb6b4eda7 Size: 4.608kB
├──  ID: a8b90a62b7a0 Size: 3.584kB
├──  ID: d20f1180ff65 Size: 213.1MB
├──  ID: b87fd3fb5764 Size: 11.78kB Top Layer of: [localhost/my_image:1.0]
└──  ID: 2ccbedb45391 Size: 2.156MB Top Layer of: [localhost/my_image:1.1]
```

1.  export images and remove them from local storage
```
$ podman save my_image:1.0 my_image:1.1 > images.tar
Copying blob cb381a32b229 done  
Copying blob af1f4e41009a done  
Copying blob 4ad6ed1a687d done  
Copying blob eb1faaca3b11 done  
Copying blob e664c070f319 done  
Copying config 4d4b043eff done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures

$ podman rmi my_image:1.0 my_image:1.1
4d4b043effd6f0f40506910ce16dab88925c58029c4c4821c94c00d44b7c46b4
Untagged: localhost/my_image:1.0
Untagged: localhost/my_image:1.1
Deleted: 3a6990719716f31429cd9230f5a7d64e141172266ed63dbe2196f6a54ba0c626
```

2. reload images and notice how things are wrong: docker.io/library/my_image:1.1 (name) and points at wrong object
```
$ podman load < images.tar
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob cb381a32b229 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob af1f4e41009a skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 4ad6ed1a687d skipped: already exists  
Copying blob eb1faaca3b11 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob e664c070f319 [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 0.0b
Copying config 4d4b043eff done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob e664c070f319 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob af1f4e41009a skipped: already exists  
Copying blob cb381a32b229 [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 0.0b
Copying blob eb1faaca3b11 [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 0.0b
Copying blob 4ad6ed1a687d [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 0.0b
Copying config 4d4b043eff done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Loaded image(s): localhost/my_image:1.0, docker.io/library/my_image:1.1

$ podman image tree my_image:1.1
Image ID: 4d4b043effd6
Tags:     [docker.io/library/my_image:1.1 localhost/my_image:1.0]
Size:     219MB
Image Layers
├──  ID: cb381a32b229 Size:  5.88MB Top Layer of: [docker.io/amd64/alpine:latest docker.io/library/alpine:latest]
├──  ID: b53bb6b4eda7 Size: 4.608kB
├──  ID: a8b90a62b7a0 Size: 3.584kB
├──  ID: d20f1180ff65 Size: 213.1MB
└──  ID: b87fd3fb5764 Size: 11.78kB Top Layer of: [docker.io/library/my_image:1.1 localhost/my_image:1.0]
```

3. For demonstration purpose, I created another tar with properly-formatted json which imports succesfully
```
$ podman rmi docker.io/library/my_image:1.1 localhost/my_image:1.0
Untagged: docker.io/library/my_image:1.1
Untagged: localhost/my_image:1.0
Deleted: 4d4b043effd6f0f40506910ce16dab88925c58029c4c4821c94c00d44b7c46b4

$ podman load < images-fixed.tar
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob e664c070f319 done  
Copying blob af1f4e41009a done  
Copying blob cb381a32b229 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 4ad6ed1a687d done  
Copying blob eb1faaca3b11 done  
Copying config 4d4b043eff done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 088ba2f14706 done  
Copying blob af1f4e41009a skipped: already exists  
Copying blob e664c070f319 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob cb381a32b229 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 4ad6ed1a687d skipped: already exists  
Copying blob eb1faaca3b11 skipped: already exists  
Copying config 3a69907197 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Loaded image(s): localhost/my_image:1.0, localhost/my_image:1.1

$ podman image tree my_image:1.1
Image ID: 3a6990719716
Tags:     [localhost/my_image:1.1]
Size:     221.1MB
Image Layers
├──  ID: cb381a32b229 Size:  5.88MB Top Layer of: [docker.io/amd64/alpine:latest docker.io/library/alpine:latest]
├──  ID: b53bb6b4eda7 Size: 4.608kB
├──  ID: a8b90a62b7a0 Size: 3.584kB
├──  ID: d20f1180ff65 Size: 213.1MB
├──  ID: b87fd3fb5764 Size: 11.78kB Top Layer of: [localhost/my_image:1.0]
└──  ID: 2ccbedb45391 Size: 2.156MB Top Layer of: [localhost/my_image:1.1]
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

Semi-unrelated: Just the weird nitpicking in me speaking, but I'm surprised oci-archive doesn't check for missing layers in archive while docker-archive does (I purposedly removed layers that I knew were already on system -- just creating empty files fools podman well enough to work. I can see the point of checking, it'd just be more coherent if both format behaved the same way)

**Output of `podman version`:**
still using git HEAD
```
Version:      3.1.0-dev
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.8
Git Commit:   0a40c5a059455693de0cc9a3f4a2a16722fc61e9
Built:        Wed Mar  3 13:43:36 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

comments.body: Thanks for reaching out.  You have to use the `-m` flag in podman-save to create a multi-image tar ball.

d'oh, that's what you meant by documented, it is indeed written there that it only works with docker archive.

There's still a weird issue with the tag name (it pulled docker.io/library from I have no idea where), but that works for me -- sorry for the noise.
